titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/252

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=234 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:18:19.816552+00 | translation moa=235 status=live modified=2021-04-06 08:18:18.00307+00

p.cl: Omnibus Sancte Matris Ecclesie filiis et fidelibus Edgarus filius Dofnaldi . Salutem.

Sciant omnes tam presentes quam futuri me Consensu filii ⁊ heredis mei
fergus dedisse ⁊ concessisse et hac presenti mea Carta confirmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie Sancte crucis de Edenburc ⁊ Canonicis ibidem deo ser
uientibus et seruituris ecclesiam de Dalgarenoc cum tota terra ad eandem ecclesiam pertinente ⁊ cum communi pastura predicte uille ⁊ omnibus
aliis rectitudinibus ⁊ pertinenciis suis tam in decimis quam in aliis ouencionibus ad eandem ecclesiam iuste pertinentibus tenendum sibi in liberam puram
⁊ perpetuam elemosinam pro salute animarum dominorum meorum Regum Scott’ ⁊ pro animabus patris mei ⁊ matris mee ⁊ omnium antecessorum ⁊ suc
cessorum meorum. Quare volo ut predicti Canonici prefatam ecclesiam cum terra ⁊ omnibus libertatibus suis ita libere quiete plenarie ⁊ honorifice
in perpetuum teneant ⁊ possideant Sicut aliqua ecclesia in toto regno scott’ liberius datur quiecius ⁊ plenius tenetur ⁊ possidetur saluo personatu .G.
persone ⁊ perpetua uicaria Enoc.

Hiis testibus fergus filio domini edgari . Martino persona de Dumf’ . Enoc clerico . Iohanne filio Radulfi decani
de Dumfr’ . Gillecrist Iudice . Gille conel ⁊ Dofnalt filiis edgari ⁊ multis aliis boni testimonii viris
To all sons and faithful people of Holy Mother Church, Edgar son of Donald sends Greeting:

Let all people, both present and future, know that, with the consent of my son and heir, Fergus, I have given and granted, and by this present charter made firm, to God and to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh, and to the canons serving God there now and in the future, the church of Dalgarnock with all the land pertaining to the same church, and with common pasture of the foresaid toun, and all its other revenues and pertinents, in teinds as well as in other obventions, justly belonging to the same church, for them to hold in free, pure and perpetual alms, for the salvation of the souls of my lords the kings of Scots, and for the souls of my father and my mother and of all my ancestors and successors. Wherefore I will that the foresaid canons hold and possess the forementioned church with its land and all its liberties so freely, quietly, fully and honourably in perpetuity as any church in the whole of the kingdom of the Scots is freely given, is quietly and fully held and possessed, reserving the parsonage of G. the parson, and the perpetual vicarage of Enoch.

With these witnesses, Fergus son of the lord Edgar, Martin the parson of Dumfries, Enoch the clerk, John son of Ralph the dean of Dumfries, Gilchrist the judex, Gilla Conaill and Donald sons of Edgar, and many other men of good testimony.
